title: Open-vocabulary bindings + restricted-terminology crosswalk stubs
context: The CDE set (cde-001) requires formal vocabulary binding files that specify which code systems serve each field. Open vocabularies (Mondo, ORDO, HPO, OncoTree, NCIt) must be the primary bindings; restricted terminologies (SNOMED CT, ICD-O-3, LOINC) may only appear as code-only crosswalk stubs for adopters who hold the relevant licences. The CI licence deny-list linter (ci-001) must remain green. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-cde-001. Standards and licence reviewer sign-off required.
objective: Produce the open-vocabulary binding layer (`vocab/`) providing: (1) explicit value-set definitions binding CDE fields to open code systems (Mondo/ORDO for disease, HPO for phenotype, OncoTree/NCIt for oncology tumour types), with all required `sources/allowlist.yml` entries in approved status; and (2) crosswalk stub tables for restricted systems (SNOMED CT, ICD-O-3, LOINC) containing only target code identifiers — no proprietary descriptions or hierarchy — so adopters who hold licences can complete the mapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Disease and phenotype bindings use Mondo (CC-BY-4.0) and Orphanet/ORDO (CC-BY-4.0) as primary disease vocabulary; HPO for phenotype.
Oncology tumour-type bindings use OncoTree (open/MSK) and NCIt (public/NCI) as primary systems.
Bindings to SNOMED CT, ICD-O-3, and LOINC are crosswalk stubs only: target codes referenced by identifier, with no proprietary descriptions, definitions, or hierarchy embedded.
Every vocabulary file carries provenance (source name, version, retrieval date, licence, allowlist entry reference) per the prov-001 model.
CI licence deny-list linter passes (no embedded restricted terminology); `pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` green.
All open vocabulary sources referenced have status: approved in `sources/allowlist.yml`; standards and licence reviewer sign-off recorded.
output: A `vocab/` directory containing open-vocabulary binding files and restricted-terminology crosswalk stub tables, with provenance metadata, committed via PR with standards and licence reviewer sign-off.
